name: Preterite / Simple past (datid)
explanation: The preterite (datid) describes completed past actions or states. Regular verbs take the ending -ede (arbejdede) or -te (spiste, mĂ¸dte) â€” there is no subject agreement. Key irregular verbs must be memorised: vĂ¦reâ†’var, gĂĄâ†’gik, sigeâ†’sagde, kommeâ†’kom, fĂĄâ†’fik, tageâ†’tog, seâ†’sĂĄ, videâ†’vidste.

examples.danish: I gĂĄr spiste vi pizza og sĂĄ en film.
examples.english: Yesterday we ate pizza and watched a film.

examples.danish: Han gik hjem, fordi han var trĂ¦t.
examples.english: He went home because he was tired.

name: Negation placement ("ikke")
explanation: In the preterite, 'ikke' (not) still comes directly after the finite verb â€” the same rule as in the present tense. It never goes before the verb.

examples.danish: Jeg sagde ikke noget pĂĄ mĂ¸det.
examples.english: I didn't say anything at the meeting.

examples.danish: Hun kom ikke til kurset i gĂĄr.
examples.english: She didn't come to the class yesterday.
